cdc_causes.name: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

items: Insect Study: Bees and other pollinators support both wild ecosystems and agricultural food production.
Inventing: U.S. utility patents generally run 20 years from filing; design patents have a 15-year term from grant.
Journalism: Strong news reporting quickly answers who, what, when, where, why, and how.
Kayaking: Length, width, hull shape, and rocker all affect speed, stability, and maneuverability.
Landscape Architecture: Sustainable design can conserve water, reuse materials, and reduce energy demand.
Law: U.S. coins and currency are legal tender for debts, though businesses may set reasonable payment policies.
Lifesaving: Protect yourself first—an untrained rescuer who enters danger can become a second victim.
Mining in Society: Scientists study asteroids partly because they preserve clues and may contain useful resources.
Motorboating: Gasoline-powered boats need proper ventilation to prevent explosive fuel-vapor buildup.
Moviemaking: Motion pictures create apparent movement by presenting a rapid sequence of still images.
Nature: Spiders are arachnids, not insects; they have eight legs and two main body sections.
Nuclear Science: ALARA means keeping radiation exposure as low as reasonably achievable.
Oceanography: Temperature and salinity help determine seawater density and drive ocean circulation.
Orienteering: UTM divides most of Earth into zones and uses meter-based grid coordinates.
Painting: A color wheel helps artists compare complementary, analogous, and other color relationships.
Pioneering: A spar is a strong pole used as a structural member in towers, bridges, and other lashings projects.
Plant Science: Agronomy focuses on field crops and soils; horticulture emphasizes fruits, vegetables, and ornamentals.
Plumbing: Safe water supply and wastewater removal are basic protections for public health.
Pottery: Electric, gas, wood, and raku kilns create different firing conditions and results.
Programming: Programmers write, test, debug, and maintain code.
Public Health: Tracking leading causes of death helps communities set prevention priorities.
Public Speaking: A rhetorical question is asked for effect rather than to obtain an answer.
Pulp and Paper: Recovered paper can be repulped and manufactured into new paper products.
Radio: AM varies a carrier wave’s amplitude; FM varies its frequency.
Railroading: End-of-train devices monitor brake-pipe conditions and replaced many caboose functions.
